sourceTitle: Grant the right level of access with teamspaces and groups

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using custom groups in Notion?

options.text: They let you quickly share content with many people at once.
options.explanation: Custom groups allow you to add an entire group to a teamspace or page in one click, making bulk sharing fast.

options.text: They automatically create new teamspaces for each member.
options.explanation: Groups do not create teamspaces; they are used to manage members within existing teamspaces.

options.text: They replace the need for individual permissions entirely.
options.explanation: While groups simplify bulk permission changes, individual overrides are still possible.

options.text: They limit a user to view‑only access across the whole workspace.
options.explanation: Groups can be given any permission level; they do not enforce view‑only by default.

questions.question: Which type of teamspace lets anyone view its content and join without an invitation?

options.text: Open
options.explanation: Open teamspaces are visible to everyone and can be joined freely.

options.text: Closed
options.explanation: Closed teamspaces are visible but require an invitation to join.

options.text: Private
options.explanation: Private teamspaces are only visible to members.

options.text: Restricted
options.explanation: ‘Restricted’ is not a teamspace classification in Notion.

questions.question: Where can you manage and edit groups in Notion?

options.text: Settings & members → Groups tab
options.explanation: Groups are managed from the Settings & members area under the Groups tab.

options.text: Teamspace settings → Members tab
options.explanation: The Members tab lets you add people to a specific teamspace, not manage groups globally.

options.text: Security tab → Permissions section
options.explanation: The Security tab controls sharing and export settings, not group management.

options.text: Workspace sidebar → Templates gallery
options.explanation: Templates are unrelated to group creation or editing.

questions.question: By default, how do page permissions behave inside a teamspace?

options.text: Pages inherit the teamspace’s permission settings unless manually changed.
options.explanation: Unless you customize a page’s permissions, it follows the teamspace’s default access level.

options.text: Pages always start with ‘Can edit’ for all members.
options.explanation: The default depends on the teamspace’s settings; it isn’t always ‘Can edit’.

options.text: Pages are private to the creator until shared.
options.explanation: Within a teamspace, pages are not private by default; they follow the teamspace’s permissions.

options.text: Pages automatically have ‘No access’ for non‑members.
options.explanation: Non‑members may have view, comment, or edit rights based on the teamspace’s open/closed settings.

questions.question: In a Closed teamspace, who can invite new members if the owner chooses ‘owners only’?

options.text: Only teamspace owners can send invitations.
options.explanation: When set to ‘owners only’, members without owner status cannot invite others.

options.text: All members can invite anyone.
options.explanation: That would be the setting for ‘all members’, not ‘owners only’.

options.text: Guests can invite other guests.
options.explanation: Guests do not have invitation privileges in Closed teamspaces.

options.text: Anyone in the workspace can join without an invitation.
options.explanation: Closed teamspaces require an invitation; they are not open to everyone.

questions.question: Which permission level lets a user edit content inside a database page without altering the database’s structure?

options.text: Can edit content
options.explanation: ‘Can edit content’ is a special level for database pages that allows editing rows but not the schema.

options.text: Can edit
options.explanation: ‘Can edit’ allows changes to the page itself, but for databases it also permits structural edits.

options.text: Full access
options.explanation: Full access includes the ability to change the database’s structure.

options.text: Can comment
options.explanation: ‘Can comment’ does not allow any editing of content.

questions.question: If a user belongs to two teamspaces—one giving ‘Can view’ and the other ‘Can comment’—what level of access will the user have on a shared page?

options.text: Can comment, because it is the most permissive level the user has.
options.explanation: Notion always grants the most permissive permission a user receives across all teamspaces.

options.text: Can view, because the first teamspace overrides the second.
options.explanation: Permissions are not ordered by teamspace; the highest level wins.

options.text: No access, because the permissions conflict.
options.explanation: Conflicting permissions do not block access; they are merged by taking the highest level.

options.text: Full access, because the user belongs to multiple teamspaces.
options.explanation: Full access is only granted if at least one teamspace provides that level.

questions.question: When you create a teamspace from an existing group, what happens to the group’s members?

options.text: All current group members are automatically added as members of the new teamspace.
options.explanation: Creating a teamspace from a group copies the group’s membership into the teamspace, saving manual effort.

options.text: Only the group owner is added to the new teamspace.
options.explanation: The process adds the entire group, not just the owner.

options.text: Group members must be invited individually after the teamspace is created.
options.explanation: The point of creating from a group is to avoid individual invitations.

options.text: The group is deleted after the teamspace is created.
options.explanation: Creating a teamspace does not delete the original group; both can coexist.

questions.question: How can you give a subset of teamspace members more access than the teamspace’s default permission?

options.text: Apply custom permissions to a group in the Members tab (Enterprise plans only).
options.explanation: Custom permissions let you grant higher access to specific groups, but this feature is limited to Enterprise plans.

options.text: Change the teamspace’s default permission for everyone.
options.explanation: Changing the default affects all members, not just a subset.

options.text: Use the Security tab to override individual member permissions.
options.explanation: The Security tab controls sharing/export settings, not granular member permissions.

options.text: Create a separate private teamspace for those members.
options.explanation: While possible, this creates a new teamspace rather than customizing permissions within the existing one.
